questions.question: In the following question, correct the equation by interchanging two signs. 17 + 80 ÷ 10 - 12 X 113 = 0
questions.explanation: On interchanging - and X sign we get, LHS = 17 + 80 ÷ 10 X 12 - 113 = 17 + 8 X 12 - 113 = 17 + 96 - 113 = 17 - 17 = 0

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number pair from the given alternatives.
In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Cobalt is a chemical element while, all others are types of rocks.

questions.question: In a certain code language, "MAGAZINE" is written as "12161259135". How is "PRIORITY" written in that code language?
questions.explanation: The numbers corresponding to the letters are written alternately such that at odd places '1' is subtracted, and at even places its written as it is. M→13 - 1 = 12 A→1 G→7 - 1 = 6 A→1 Z→26 - 1 = 25 I→9 N→14 - 1 = 13 E→5 Similarly, P→16 - 1 = 15 R→18 I→9 - 1 = 8 O→15 R→18 - 1 = 17 I→9 T→20 - 1 = 19 Y→25 So, PRIORITY → 15188151791925

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Except option (b), all follow the following pattern: S - 4 = O Reverse of S → H Reverse of O → L V - 4 = R Reverse of V → E Reverse of R → I E - 4 = A Reverse of E → V Reverse of A → Z In option (b), J - 4 = F, which is given as G, hence odd.

questions.question: The series is given, with one term missing. Choose amongst the given responses and complete the series. RUX, MPS, ILO, FIL, __.
questions.explanation: 1ˢᵗ letter of each term; R - 5 = M M - 4 = I I - 3 = F F - 2 = D 2ⁿᵈ letter of each term; U - 5 = P P - 4 = L L - 3 = I I - 2 = G 3ʳᵈ letter of each term; X - 5 = S S - 4 = O O - 3 = L L - 2 = J

questions.question: In a row of 39 persons Jaya was 12ᵗʰ to the left of Raj who was 12ᵗʰ from the right end. If Suresh is 8ᵗʰ from the left end and Farhan is exactly between Jaya and Suresh, then what is Farhan's rank from the left end of the row. Assume people were standing facing the North.
questions.explanation: Raj was 12ᵗʰ from the right hence Jaya would be 24ᵗʰ from the right Position of Jaya from the left= 39-24+1= 39-23= 16ᵗʰ Suresh's rank =8ᵗʰ from left Farhan's rank = (16+8)/2 =12ᵗʰ from the left

questions.question: What was the day of the week on 26ᵗʰ January 1950?
questions.explanation: Last 2 digits of the year= 50 Quotient on dividing 50 by 4= 12 Date=26 Month Code= 0 (January) Year Code= 0 (1900-1999) Total Sum= (50+12+26+0+0) = 88 Remainder on dividing 88 by 7= 4 Day code 4 is for Thursday

questions.question: Choose a figure which would most closely resemble the unfolded form of figures after folding and cutting. Problem figure ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/6700/image014.png) Answer figures ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/6700/image015.png)
questions.explanation: After first unfold, the right half will have two circles and a triangle. After final unfold, there will be four circles in the centre and two triangles near the two vertices.

questions.question: Some footballers are defenders. All defenders are swift. All strikers are swift. Conclusion I: All strikers are defenders. Conclusion II: Some swift are footballers. Conclusion III: Some strikers may be footballers. Conclusion IV: No striker is a defender.

questions.question: In each of the following questions, choose the correct mirror image of the figure from amongst the four alternatives. The mirror may be represented by a line MN. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/2184/image010.png) Option Figures ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/sscimg/2184/image011.png)
questions.explanation: Since the mirror is placed horizontally, the image will be flipped along the horizontal plane. The triangle which was pointing bottom right will now point top right.

questions.question: Select the missing number from the given options. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/89662/image004.jpg)

Abinoy walks 5m towards his north-east (exactly 45⁰) in order to reach Priyanka, who then has to walk 10m towards her east to reach Rajat. Rajat then walks 5m towards south in order to reach Panchlata. Find the shortest distance between the initial positions of Priyanka and Panchlata.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/s3018.jpg) The shortest distance is √10² +5² = √125 = 11.18 m ≈ 11.2 m

questions.question: A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as in two matrices given below. The columns and rows of Matrix I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices be represented first by its row and next by its column. e.g. 'A' can be represented by 01, 14 etc. and 'M' can be represented by 56, 68 etc. Similarly, you have identify the set for the word 'STANCH'. (MATRIX) MATRIX I 01234 0MCDST 1CTSMD 2DMTCS 3SDMTC 4TSCDM MATRIX II 56789 5AGHNL 6LNGAH 7GHALN 8NALHG 9HLNGA
questions.explanation: From the matrix, we have S = 03,12,24,30,41 T = 04,11,22,33,40 A = 55,68,77,86,99 N = 58,66,79,85,97 C =01,10,23,34,42 H = 57, 69, 76, 88, 95 Only the option 12,22,99,66,23,88 has the codes of the letters in the same sequence.

questions.question: Arrange the following words as per order in the dictionary 1. Rebellion 2. Reconnaissance 3. Recapitulate 4. Recuperate 5. Rebarbative 6. Recrudesce 7. Recalcitrant
questions.explanation: The correct order is: 5. Rebarbative 1. Rebellion 7. Recalcitrant 3. Recapitulate 2. Reconnaissance 6. Recrudesce 4. Recuperate

questions.question: From the given alternative words, select the one which cannot be formed using the letters of the given word only once INTERROGATION
questions.explanation: Only the word ROAD cannot be formed by using the given word. Because there is no letter D in the given word.

questions.question: Pointing to a photograph, Prakash said "Her father's only son is the father of my brother". How is that person in the photograph related to Prakash's father?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/70628/image001.jpg) The person in the photograph is the sister of Prakash's father.

questions.question: ERHM : IQLL : : CPJT : ?
questions.explanation: ERHM CPJT +3-1+3-1 +3-1+3-1 IQLL GONS
